summary: Ukrainian FPV drones struck Russian equipment on a highway in the Pisky area of occupied Donetsk, with Russian milbloggers noting Ukrainian drone dominance now extends into deep rear areas, threatening supply lines. Ukrainian forces are increasing use of "Molniya" strike and reconnaissance drones, but electronic warfare systems struggle to counter them due to unknown control frequencies. Russian BM-35 and BM-70 drones that previously attacked Sumy and other cities have largely stopped, possibly due to production issues, intensified Ukrainian electronic warfare and interceptor drones, or the disconnection of Starlink systems used for control. The Russian "Rubicon" Center reported 114 targets destroyed on March 9 and 162 on March 10, including strikes on Ukrainian personnel, FPV air defense against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, and joint operations with Aerospace Forces to target Ukrainian UAV control centers.
